LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT SANFORD ROSEN NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO DISMISS OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, TO STAY. A R G U E D. THE COURT HAVING REVIEWED THE PLEADINGS AND HAVING HEARD ORAL ARGUMENT RULES AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT SANFORD ROSEN'S MOTION TO STAY THIS CASE PENDING THE CONCLUSION OF PROCEEDINGS IN NEW YORK ON THE CLAIMS ALLEGED BY PLAINTIFF CHIKAKO HALL IS GRANTED. THE SUPREME COURT OF NEW YORK IS THE PROPER FORUM, AT LEAST IN THE FIRST INSTANCE, FOR THE ADJUDICATION OF MS. HALL'S CLAIMS AGAINST MR. ROSEN. MR. ROSEN WAS APPOINTED AS AN ASSIGNEE PER A NEW YORK STATUTORY SCHEME AND THE NEW YORK COURT HAS SUPERVISORY AUTHORITY OVER MR. ROSEN, WHICH INCLUDES THE AUTHORITY TO IMPOSE PERSONAL LIABILITY AGAINST MR. ROSEN FOR HIS CONDUCT RELATING TO HIS ROLE AS ASSIGNEE. WHILE CALIFORNIA HAS AN INTEREST IN UPHOLDING THE ENFORCEMENT OF ITS LAWS AND MS. HALL IS A CALIFORNIA RESIDENT, PRINCIPLES OF COMITY REQUIRE THAT THE NEW YORK COURT, NOT THIS COURT, ADJUDICATE MS. HALL'S CLAIMS. THERE HAS BEEN NO SHOWING THAT THE NEW YORK COURT IS EITHER UNABLE OR UNWILLING TO ADJUDICATE THOSE CLAIMS OR WILL REFUSE TO APPLY CALIFORNIA LAW IF DETERMINED THAT CALIFORNIA LAW APPLIES. Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss Or, In The Alternative, To Stay
Off Calendar Feb-13-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Ara Jabagchourian, Esq, Ara Law, Telephone 650-437-6840, appearing for Plaintiff;
Benjamin C. Koodrich, Es., Murphy Pearson Bradley & feeney, telephone 415-788-1900

Ruling - A R G U E D. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT SANFORD ROSEN Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss Or, In The Alternative, To Stay

DEFENDANT SANFORD ROSEN Motion To Dismiss Or, In The Alternative, To Stay

Defendant Sanford Rosen's motion to stay this case pending the conclusion of proceedings in New York on the claims alleged by plaintiff Chikako Hall is granted. The Supreme Court of New York is the proper forum, at least in the first instance, for the adjudication of Ms. Hall's claims against Mr. Rosen. Mr. Rosen was appointed as an assignee per a New York statutory scheme and the New York court has supervisory authority over Mr. Rosen, which includes the authority to impose personal liability against Mr. Rosen for his conduct relating to his role as assignee. While California has an interest in upholding the enforcement of its laws and Ms. Hall is a California resident, principles of comity require that the New York court, not this court, adjudicate Ms. Hall's claims. There has been no showing that the New York court is either unable or unwilling to adjudicate those claims or will refuse to apply California law if determined that California law applies.

order signed.

Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Clerk: W. Trupek; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano CSR# 8600 email: maria.torreano@gmail.com (302/HEK)
